prerequisites: An Outreach account with access to the Outreach development portal (https://developers.outreach.io) where you can create an app.
A registered OAuth application in the Outreach development portal. On the app's API access tab, select at least one OAuth data scope and specify at least one redirect URI.
The Application Identifier (client_id) and Application Secret (client_secret) issued for your app. The client secret is displayed only once when generated, so record it immediately.
A redirect_uri that exactly matches one of the redirect URIs you registered on the app.
A refresh_token obtained by completing the OAuth 2.0 authorization code grant against your Outreach app.
A start_date marking the earliest point from which the connector should begin syncing data.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (authorization code grant with refresh token)
authMethods.summary: Outreach authenticates via OAuth 2.0. You register an app to receive a client_id (Application Identifier) and client_secret (Application Secret), direct a user to https://api.outreach.io/oauth/authorize to grant the selected scopes, and exchange the returned authorization code at https://api.outreach.io/oauth/token for an access token and a refresh_token. The connector uses client_id, client_secret, redirect_uri, and refresh_token to mint fresh access tokens. Access tokens expire after 2 hours, so the connector refreshes them at the token endpoint using grant_type=refresh_token.

setupSteps.title: Create an Outreach OAuth application
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Outreach development portal at https://developers.outreach.io and create an app. On the app's API access tab, select at least one OAuth data scope and specify at least one redirect URI. Scopes are period-separated strings of a pluralized resource name and a permission token (for example prospects.read).

setupSteps.title: Record the client_id and client_secret
setupSteps.body: After creating the app, Outreach issues an Application Identifier (use as client_id) and an Application Secret (use as client_secret). The client secret is shown only once at generation time, so copy and store it securely before leaving the page.

setupSteps.title: Authorize and capture an authorization code
setupSteps.body: Send a user to https://api.outreach.io/oauth/authorize with response_type=code, your client_id, a registered redirect_uri, and the requested scopes. After the user authorizes, Outreach redirects to redirect_uri with a code parameter.

setupSteps.title: Exchange the code for a refresh token
setupSteps.body: POST to https://api.outreach.io/oauth/token with grant_type=authorization_code, your client_id, client_secret, redirect_uri, and the authorization code. The response contains an access token and a refresh_token. Save the refresh_token for the connector.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter client_id, client_secret, redirect_uri, and refresh_token in the connector configuration, then set start_date to the earliest date from which data should be synced. The connector uses these values to obtain fresh access tokens via grant_type=refresh_token as needed.
